project_structure: Fabric is a pnpm monorepo with three packages: packages/shared (Zod schemas + i18n + types), packages/server (services layer including doctor, event-ledger, knowledge-meta-builder, knowledge-sync), and packages/cli (citty-based fab CLI with commands/{doctor,scan,install,uninstall,serve,config,plan-context-hint}.ts). For rc.20 Cite-coverage the relevant flow is: knowledge frontmatter (relevance_paths) → knowledge-meta-builder.ts writes .fabric/agents.meta.json → events.jsonl appended by appendEventLedgerEvent → readEventLedger filters by event_type/since → doctor.ts inspect* + create* check pipeline → cli/commands/doctor.ts renders via writeIssueSection + i18n keys. The coverage algorithm must hook into all five layers.

relevant_files.rationale: Defines ruleDescriptionSchema (lines 32-56) including relevance_scope ('narrow'|'broad', default 'broad') and relevance_paths (string[], default []) — these are the EXACT fields the coverage denominator must replay to compute 'edits this cite should apply to'. Knowledge entries with empty relevance_paths fall into the broad-entry bucket; narrow entries with paths drive per-edit denominator filtering.

key_code.description: Zod z.array(z.string()).default([]) — the field replayed against the project's edit set to compute per-cite denominator. Empty array means broad/no-anchor.
z.enum(['narrow','broad']).default('broad') — narrow + non-empty paths → denominator filter; broad → counts 'covers any edit' bucket.
Top-level schema with nodes record keyed by stable_id; coverage replay iterates meta.nodes and reads node.description.relevance_paths for each KP-/KT- entry.

relevant_files.topic_relation: coverage-algo perspective: this schema is the ground truth for the denominator computation. The cite-coverage algorithm starts here — without a typed relevance_paths field, the denominator 'edits this cite should apply to' is undefined.

relevant_files.rationale: Builds .fabric/agents.meta.json. extractKnowledgeFieldsFromFrontmatter() (L933-L1034) reads relevance_paths from each knowledge entry's frontmatter via extractInlineArray (L1021). buildKnowledgeMeta is what produces the meta.json snapshot the coverage replay loads — any algorithm consuming relevance_paths must trust this builder's output.

key_code.description: Parses frontmatter into KnowledgeFrontmatterFields including relevance_scope (L1011-L1015) and relevance_paths (L1021 via extractInlineArray flow-style YAML).
Entry point that produces { meta, knowledgeTestIndex, changed }. Coverage replay would call this (or readExistingMeta L267) to get the canonical relevance_paths snapshot.
Regex parser for flow-style YAML arrays e.g. `relevance_paths: [src/foo.ts, src/bar/]` — used for the relevance_paths field at L1021.

relevant_files.topic_relation: coverage-algo perspective: the denominator depends on accurate relevance_paths extraction. This file is the only producer of meta.description.relevance_paths; bugs here directly corrupt the coverage denominator.

relevant_files.rationale: Owns events.jsonl read/append. readEventLedger (L52-L105) is exactly the API the cite-coverage report will use — it already accepts { event_type, since, correlation_id, session_id } filter options. Line-by-line full-file read with partial-write tail detection (L73-L92); coverage replay can reuse this without reinventing parsing.

key_code.description: Full-file read then split + per-line JSON.parse via parseEventLedgerLine (L145-L160). Supports filtering by event_type/since (epoch ms)/correlation_id/session_id — exactly the surface needed for --since=7d and per-client filtering.
Append API used to write cite_policy_activated marker (rc.20 install hook) and per-cite events (cite_planned/cite_recalled/cite_dismissed).

Filter shape: only allows ONE event_type at a time — coverage replay will need multiple passes (or a new options.event_types[] overload) to gather cite_planned + cite_recalled + cite_dismissed in one sweep.

relevant_files.topic_relation: coverage-algo perspective: this is the runtime data source for the coverage numerator (cite events). The 'pre-policy marker' requirement (cite_policy_activated as turn-boundary anchor) means the replay must filter events by ts >= marker_ts — which readEventLedger's `since` option already supports.

relevant_files.rationale: Defines the discriminated union eventLedgerEventSchema (L397-L443). rc.20 must add four NEW event schemas: cite_policy_activated (the pre-policy marker), cite_planned, cite_recalled, cite_dismissed (with reason enum scope-mismatch/outdated/not-applicable/other:<text>). Existing edit_intent_checked (L53-L68) is the candidate event for the denominator's 'edit' definition since it ALREADY records path + ts per edit.

key_code.description: Already records { path, compliant, intent, ledger_entry_id, matched_rule_context_ts, window_ms } per AI edit. Candidate source for 'edit触达数' denominator — each event = one edit to which cite policy applies.
Records final_stable_ids + ai_selected_stable_ids per fetch — candidate source for 'recalled but not verified' bucket (cite was pulled by AI but no cite event ever emitted).

Discriminated union — rc.20 must add cite_planned/cite_recalled/cite_dismissed/cite_policy_activated to this array (mirroring the L429/L431/L434/L437/L439/L442 comment pattern documenting each new schema's purpose).
Reference shape for a heartbeat-style event: { event_type, mode, issues, mutations?, timestamp }. cite_policy_activated marker should mirror this minimal shape (just timestamp + version).

relevant_files.topic_relation: coverage-algo perspective: the event schema additions ARE the cite-coverage data model. The reason enum (scope-mismatch/outdated/not-applicable/other:<text>) lives here; the marker shape lives here. Without these schemas, no replay is possible.

relevant_files.rationale: runDoctorReport (L746-L954) is the entry point for `fab doctor`. Existing buildLastActiveIndex pattern (L2885-L2950) at L2891 reads events.jsonl, switches on event_type, accumulates per-stable_id timestamps — this is the EXACT pattern cite-coverage replay would mirror. The Promise.all inspect block (L750-L772) is where a new inspectCiteCoverage would slot in.

key_code.description: Existing entry. Adding --cite-coverage as a flag → augment this function; as a subcommand → split into runDoctorCiteCoverage. The Promise.all block at L750-L772 is where an inspectCiteCoverage(projectRoot, { since, client }) call would land.

Reference pattern for event-stream replay: single readEventLedger pass, switch on event_type accumulating into Map<stableId, ts>. Cite-coverage replay mirrors this: switch on cite_planned|cite_recalled|cite_dismissed and accumulate per-cite Map<stableId, CiteOutcome[]>.
Report shape: { status, checks, fixable_errors, manual_errors, warnings, infos, summary }. Cite-coverage either extends summary (new field) or returns a parallel CiteCoverageReport type.
Best-effort append idiom — cite_policy_activated marker emission at install time MUST use this same try/catch swallow pattern (observability writes never propagate failures).
Existing example of an inspection that reads events.jsonl and synthesizes findings. Cite-coverage inspection should slot adjacent to this in the Promise.all block.

relevant_files.topic_relation: coverage-algo perspective: doctor.ts is the host for the coverage algorithm output. The replay pattern (buildLastActiveIndex) is already proven in this file — the cite-coverage replay is a structural twin.

relevant_files.rationale: CLI surface for fab doctor — defineCommand args block (L71-L109) is where --cite-coverage, --since, --client flags are added. renderHumanReport (L249-L257) is where coverage section rendering hooks in (a new writeIssueSection call). emitDoctorRunEventBestEffort (L301-L316) is the template for cite event emission idioms.

key_code.description: citty args block. Adding `cite-coverage: { type: 'boolean' }`, `since: { type: 'string', default: '7d' }`, `client: { type: 'string', default: 'all' }` follows the existing --fix-knowledge / --rescan pattern verbatim.
Formatter — adds writeIssueSection(t('doctor.cite.section.summary'), ...) and per-metric line writers (edit触达/合格cite/应查没查/...) here.
Existing helper — reusable for cite-coverage section rendering. Same shape: title + bullet list.
Args interface — add `'cite-coverage'?: boolean; since?: string; client?: 'cc'|'codex'|'cursor'|'all'`. Naming convention bracketed for kebab-case (mirrors 'fix-knowledge').

relevant_files.topic_relation: coverage-algo perspective: the CLI is where the user actually invokes the algorithm. The --since=7d / --client=cc|codex|cursor|all surface from the locked spec must be wired through this file.

relevant_files.rationale: Doctor i18n keys live at L140-L163 (cli.doctor.*) and L148-L151 (doctor.section.*). Pattern is clear: cli.doctor.args.{flag}.description for flag descriptions, doctor.section.{name} for output section headers. rc.20 must add: cli.doctor.args.cite-coverage.description, cli.doctor.args.since.description, cli.doctor.args.client.description, doctor.section.cite-coverage, doctor.cite.metric.* (中文术语 + English), doctor.cite.dismissed-reason.* (4 enum values).

key_code.description: Insertion point pattern. Add cite-coverage/since/client keys here following the same `Apply knowledge lint mutations: ...` prose style.
Section header pattern. Add doctor.section.cite-coverage = 'Cite coverage:' here.
Top-level help text — must add `fab doctor --cite-coverage  cite policy adherence report` line.

relevant_files.topic_relation: coverage-algo perspective: the report metrics (中文+English bilingual per locked spec: 'Edit 触达数 / KB 应有相关条目的 edit / 合格 cite / recalled 但未验证 / 应查没查') and dismissed-reason enum labels MUST live in i18n for the bilingual locked decision to be honored.

relevant_files.rationale: Chinese mirror of en.ts. rc.20 doctor.cite.* keys must be added symmetrically — locked spec uses 中文 metric names (Edit 触达数 / 应查没查 / 合格 cite). The zh-CN locale carries the canonical Chinese phrasing for those.
relevant_files.topic_relation: coverage-algo perspective: bilingual reporting is a locked decision. Without zh-CN.ts symmetry, the 中文 metric names from the spec cannot be rendered when locale is zh-CN.

relevant_files.rationale: Test seeding pattern for events.jsonl is established: writeFile('.fabric/events.jsonl', '', target) for empty (L91, L197, L222...) or writeFileSync(ledgerPath, `${legacyEvent}\n`, 'utf8') for seeded events (L394-L395, L426, L850). createInitializedProject helper sets up the .fabric layout. New cite-coverage tests follow this exact pattern: seed events.jsonl with cite_planned/cite_recalled/cite_dismissed lines, then assert report.cite_coverage fields.

key_code.description: Test helper for seeding fixture files. Used uniformly to seed empty events.jsonl in nearly every test (L91, L197, L222, L253, L274, L306, L360, L407, L423...).
writeFileSync(ledgerPath, `${event_json}\n`, 'utf8') — newline-terminated JSON lines. Cite-coverage tests seed { kind:'fabric-event', event_type:'cite_planned', ts, ... } lines this way.
beforeEach sets FABRIC_HOME to a tmpdir so personal knowledge root doesn't leak. Cite-coverage tests inherit this isolation automatically.

relevant_files.topic_relation: coverage-algo perspective: test seeding pattern confirms the replay algorithm can be exercised purely via filesystem fixtures (no MCP server / runtime client needed). Critical for TDD of the algorithm.

relevant_files.rationale: Live baseline showing the actual on-disk shape: 16 canonical KP-/KT- entries, each with description.relevance_paths (currently [] for all — broad scope). Confirms the empty-relevance_paths default behavior the algorithm must handle correctly (broad-entry bucket).

key_code.description: Real example: [] — empty array because the entry pre-dates Phase-1.5 and was never given path anchors. Coverage algorithm denominator for this entry: 'covers any edit' bucket.

relevant_files.topic_relation: coverage-algo perspective: validates the algorithm against the actual baseline shape. All 16 current entries fall into the broad bucket — the narrow path-filtered denominator will only kick in once users start declaring relevance_paths in frontmatter.

relevant_files.rationale: Live event ledger from the running project — confirms real event shape: { kind:'fabric-event', id, ts, schema_version:1, event_type, ... }. Shows init_scan_completed, knowledge_drift_detected, etc. The cite_policy_activated marker emission must produce a line matching this canonical shape.
relevant_files.topic_relation: coverage-algo perspective: confirms the on-disk event line format the replay parser must accept. The 'pre-policy marker' filter is `events.filter(e => e.ts >= cite_policy_activated.ts)` — a simple numeric comparison on the existing ts field.

relevant_files.rationale: Sample knowledge file showing the actual frontmatter shape parsed by knowledge-meta-builder. Confirms which fields ARE present (id/type/maturity/layer/layer_reason/created_at/tags) and which are ABSENT (relevance_scope/relevance_paths — defaulted to broad/[] by the parser).
relevant_files.topic_relation: coverage-algo perspective: confirms most existing entries have no explicit relevance_paths declaration → they all enter the broad-entry denominator bucket until users opt into narrow scope.

patterns: 1) Event ledger replay pattern (doctor.ts L2885-L2950 buildLastActiveIndex): single readEventLedger pass → switch on event_type → accumulate into Map<stableId, ts>. Cite-coverage replay is a structural twin — switch on cite_planned/recalled/dismissed → accumulate per-cite outcomes. 2) Best-effort event emission (cli/commands/doctor.ts L301-L316 emitDoctorRunEventBestEffort): try/catch swallow — observability writes never propagate failures. Cite-coverage event emissions follow this verbatim. 3) Doctor inspection pattern (doctor.ts inspect* family): each inspect{Name} is a pure async function returning { candidates: [...] }, then create{Name}Check converts the inspection into a DoctorCheck with kind/status/message/actionHint. 4) i18n keys (shared/i18n/locales/en.ts + zh-CN.ts): cli.{cmd}.* for CLI flag descriptions, doctor.section.{name} for output headers, both locales kept symmetric. 5) Frontmatter parsing (knowledge-meta-builder.ts L1036-L1057): regex-based scalar + flow-style array extraction, default-safe (missing → schema default, malformed → default + stderr warn). 6) Test seeding (doctor.test.ts): writeFile/writeFileSync for fixtures, createInitializedProject for full .fabric scaffolding, FABRIC_HOME isolation per test.
dependencies: Server services depend on @fenglimg/fabric-shared for Zod schemas (event-ledger.ts, agents-meta.ts) and on @fenglimg/fabric-shared/node for atomic-write + framework detection. doctor.ts imports from knowledge-meta-builder.ts (buildKnowledgeMeta, isSameKnowledgeTestIndex, writeKnowledgeMeta), event-ledger.ts (appendEventLedgerEvent, readEventLedger, truncateLedgerToLastNewline), knowledge-sync.ts (reconcileKnowledge), _shared.ts (ensureParentDirectory, getEventLedgerPath, sha256). CLI doctor.ts depends on @fenglimg/fabric-server for the full doctor surface + checkLockOrThrow. New cite-coverage code adds NO new external dependencies — every needed primitive (event read, frontmatter parse, meta load) already exists in server services.
integration_points: 1) Schema additions — packages/shared/src/schemas/event-ledger.ts L397-L443 (eventLedgerEventSchema discriminated union): add citePolicyActivatedEventSchema + citePlannedEventSchema + citeRecalledEventSchema + citeDismissedEventSchema (with reason enum). 2) Marker emission at install — packages/cli/src/commands/install.ts (one-time appendEventLedgerEvent({event_type:'cite_policy_activated', ts, version}) call on rc.20 install). 3) Coverage replay — new packages/server/src/services/cite-coverage.ts mirroring buildLastActiveIndex (doctor.ts L2885-L2950) shape: single readEventLedger pass filtered by ts >= marker_ts, switch on cite_* event_type, build per-cite Map<stableId, { planned, recalled_unverified, dismissed[], outcomes[] }>. 4) Denominator computation — replay agents.meta.json relevance_paths via minimatch (already a doctor.ts dep, L8) against edit_intent_checked event paths to produce per-cite 'edit触达' counts. 5) Doctor entry — packages/server/src/services/doctor.ts L746 runDoctorReport: either a new runDoctorCiteCoverage parallel function (recommended) or augment via args.coverage (mixed concerns). 6) CLI flags — packages/cli/src/commands/doctor.ts L71-L109 args block: add cite-coverage:boolean, since:string='7d', client:string='all' (cc|codex|cursor|all). 7) Renderer — packages/cli/src/commands/doctor.ts L249-L257 renderHumanReport: add a new writeIssueSection(t('doctor.section.cite-coverage'), ...) plus a custom per-metric writer for the 5 numeric metrics + cite-source distribution + dismissed-reason histogram. 8) i18n — packages/shared/src/i18n/locales/{en,zh-CN}.ts: add ~15 keys (3 flag descriptions, 1 section header, 5 metric labels, 4 dismissed-reason labels, 2-3 misc helpers). 9) Tests — packages/server/src/services/cite-coverage.test.ts (new file) + extend doctor.test.ts for CLI integration; seed events.jsonl via writeFileSync pattern at doctor.test.ts L426/L850.
constraints: 1) readEventLedger reads the WHOLE file into memory (L60) — fine for v2.0 ledgers (<10k events typical per the L2884 comment) but cite-coverage replay must NOT add a second full-file pass; reuse a single readEventLedger() call and switch on all relevant event_types in one loop. 2) Discriminated union (event-ledger.ts L397-L443) means adding cite_* schemas requires touching the union AND exporting their TS types (L445-L449 pattern); both locales must update keys atomically with schema. 3) `since` filter on readEventLedger (L100) is epoch-ms numeric; --since=7d CLI flag must be parsed (Date.now() - 7*86400_000). Reuse no existing parser — none exists for human duration strings in the codebase. 4) Pre-policy marker semantics: doctor only counts events where ts >= max(cite_policy_activated.ts in the ledger). If no marker exists, cite-coverage report status='skipped' (cite policy not yet activated for this workspace) — must NOT error. 5) Turn boundary (assistant ts → next user ts) requires events tagged with role/turn boundaries — current schema does NOT expose these. cite_planned/cite_recalled must include enough turn context (correlation_id? session_id? new turn_id field?) to derive the boundary at replay time. THIS IS A LIKELY CLARIFICATION POINT. 6) Reason enum 'other:<text>' is a free-form suffix — Zod schema should accept z.string().refine(s => s in fixed_set || s.startsWith('other:')) to preserve typed enum semantics. 7) Per-client split (cc/codex/cursor) requires events to carry a client identifier. Existing knowledgeConsumedEvent has client_hash (event-ledger.ts L297); cite_* events should mirror this — but client name (cc/codex/cursor) is NOT directly the hash. Need either client_id field or a hash→name mapping. 8) Doctor inspections are synchronous-ish (sequenced via Promise.all at runDoctorReport L750); cite-coverage replay must not block longer than ~100ms for typical ledgers (no synchronous large-file parses on the hot path).

clarification_needs.question: How is the turn boundary (assistant ts → next user ts) detected from the existing event ledger? Current schema doesn't expose role/turn markers — does rc.20 add a turn_id field on cite_* events, or reuse correlation_id/session_id?
clarification_needs.context: Locked spec says: 'Turn boundary: assistant message ts → next user message ts (NOT 30-second window)'. But events.jsonl today contains no user/assistant role tags — events come from hook scripts (PreToolUse/Stop) and skill invocations. Without an explicit turn marker, the replay cannot draw the assistant→user boundary needed to attribute cites to the correct edit batch.
options: Add turn_id (uuid) field to all cite_* + edit_intent_checked events; emit at session boundary by the client hook
Reuse correlation_id (already on envelope) — group events by correlation_id as the turn unit
Use session_id + monotonic ts ordering only — declare 'turn' as 'edit-or-cite events bracketed by the same session_id between fetch events'
Defer turn-boundary precision to rc.21; rc.20 uses 30-second fallback window with an explicit roadmap note

clarification_needs.question: How does the cite-coverage report distinguish per-client buckets (cc/codex/cursor)? knowledgeConsumedEvent has client_hash but no client name field.
clarification_needs.context: Locked spec says: 'CLI: fab doctor --cite-coverage [--since=7d] [--client=cc|codex|cursor|all]' and metric 按客户端拆分. But the only client identifier currently in events.jsonl is client_hash (a hashed pid+hostname-style identifier in knowledge_consumed). The mapping from {cc, codex, cursor} → client_hash is not currently maintained anywhere.
options: Add client_kind: z.enum(['cc','codex','cursor']) to cite_* events directly, emitted by each client's hook so the mapping is recorded at write time
Maintain a separate .fabric/.cache/client-registry.json that maps client_hash → client_kind, populated when each client first writes
Use file path heuristics — cc events come from .claude/hooks/, codex from .codex/hooks/, cursor from .cursor/hooks/ — and record source_path on the event

clarification_needs.question: Should --cite-coverage be a NEW subcommand (fab doctor cite-coverage) or a flag on the existing doctor command (fab doctor --cite-coverage)?
clarification_needs.context: Existing pattern: doctor.ts has flags --fix, --fix-knowledge, --rescan, --json, --strict — all flags, no subcommands. citty supports both. A flag means the cite-coverage output piggybacks on runDoctorReport (which always runs all 28+ inspections — possibly wasteful when the user only wants cite coverage). A subcommand means a parallel runDoctorCiteCoverage entry that does NOT run the full inspect block.
options: Flag (--cite-coverage) augmenting runDoctorReport — minimal CLI surface change, matches the rest of doctor's design
Subcommand (fab doctor cite-coverage) — clean separation, allows fast-path that skips the 28-check pipeline
Flag that suppresses the normal report — `fab doctor --cite-coverage` runs ONLY the coverage replay, skipping inspections (gives subcommand-speed via flag)

_metadata.task_description: Execute coverage-algo exploration for rc.20 Cite policy planning — denominator replay via relevance_paths, turn-boundary semantics, pre-policy marker, dismissed reason enum, events.jsonl read pattern, doctor surface, i18n insertion points
